message:
chore(engine): sync concurrent agent changes to demotion controller tests and beads

- Update test assertions in frx_online_regret_change_point_demotion_controller
  test suite (concurrent agent refinements)
- Sync .beads/issues.jsonl with latest status updates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -305,7 +305,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2mds.1.1.2","title":"[PSRP-01.2] Freeze canonical AST schema + hash contract (versioned)","description":"## Context
Define canonical AST serialization, hash normalization rules, and schema versioning to prevent silent semantic drift.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.","acceptance_criteria":"Schema/version contract is published, tested, and enforced in CI with compatibility checks.

Required verification addendum: include focused unit tests and, for any cross-subsystem behavior, deterministic e2e scenarios with structured logs/traces and one-command replay instructions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:01.851292671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T01:08:19.590457366Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T01:08:19.590433632Z","close_reason":"Completed canonical AST schema/hash contract freeze with versioned constants, compatibility tests, and published schema doc.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser-frontier","parser-supremacy","world-class-parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:01.851292671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:16.361789496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":244,"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented canonical AST schema/hash contract freeze for `bd-2mds.1.1.2`.

## Scope completed
- Added versioned canonical AST contract constants and metadata accessors in `crates/franken-engine/src/ast.rs`:
  - `CANONICAL_AST_CONTRACT_VERSION`
  - `CANONICAL_AST_SCHEMA_VERSION`
  - `CANONICAL_AST_HASH_ALGORITHM`
  - `CANONICAL_AST_HASH_PREFIX`
  - `SyntaxTree::{canonical_contract_version, canonical_schema_version, canonical_hash_algorithm, canonical_hash_prefix}`
- Updated `SyntaxTree::canonical_hash()` to use the pinned hash-prefix constant.
- Added compatibility/contract tests in `crates/franken-engine/tests/parser_trait_ast.rs`:
  - stable contract metadata test
  - stable canonical-hash vector tests (script signed numeric literal, module default import, module default export)
- Added contract tests in `crates/franken-engine/tests/ast_integration.rs`:
  - constants pinned
  - accessors stable
  - hash-prefix consistency
- Published canonical contract doc:
  - `docs/PARSER_CANONICAL_AST_SCHEMA.md`
  - includes canonical map schema, versioning policy, compatibility policy, and replay commands.
- Wired documentation pointer in `README.md` parser phase section.

## Verification evidence
Focused compatibility tests (via `rch`):
- Command:
  - `rch exec -- env RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_engine_parser_ast_contract cargo test -p frankenengine-engine --test parser_trait_ast --test ast_integration`
- Result:
  - `ast_integration`: 126 passed, 0 failed
  - `parser_trait_ast`: 7 passed, 0 failed

Deterministic parser gate artifact with structured logs/traces and replay contract:
- Command:
  - `./scripts/run_parser_phase0_gate.sh ci`
- Manifest:
  - `artifacts/parser_phase0_gate/20260225T005203Z/run_manifest.json`
- Structured logs:
  - `artifacts/parser_phase0_gate/20260225T005203Z/events.jsonl`

## Global gate note
Attempted workspace-level `cargo check --all-targets`, `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, and `cargo fmt --check` via `rch`; current failures are in unrelated pre-existing files/lints/format drift outside this bead’s touched surface.

Given acceptance criteria for this bead, the canonical AST schema/version contract is now published, compatibility-tested, and enforced in CI-facing parser test lanes.
","created_at":"2026-02-25T01:08:16Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2mds.1.1.3","title":"[PSRP-01.3] Build deterministic diagnostics taxonomy + normalization layer","description":"## Context
Stabilize diagnostic categories/codes/messages/spans so cross-engine and cross-run comparisons are meaningful.

## Work
- Define deterministic diagnostics taxonomy.
- Normalize parser diagnostics into canonical envelopes for comparison/testing.
- Keep diagnostics lane runnable in parallel with AST hash-contract work while preserving semantic alignment.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.","acceptance_criteria":"Diagnostics output is normalized, deterministic, and asserted in test suites and artifact schemas; unit tests and e2e diagnostics scenarios are comprehensive; structured logs make diagnostic regressions replayable and triage-friendly.

Required verification addendum: include focused unit tests and, for any cross-subsystem behavior, deterministic e2e scenarios with structured logs/traces and one-command replay instructions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:02.206864239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T02:29:23.451609678Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T02:29:23.451586134Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser-frontier","parser-supremacy","world-class-parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:02.206864239Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:55:32.719420704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":246,"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed deterministic diagnostics taxonomy + normalization contract for `bd-2mds.1.1.3`.

## Implementation
- `crates/franken-engine/src/parser.rs`
  - Added diagnostics taxonomy contract constants:
    - `PARSER_DIAGNOSTIC_TAXONOMY_VERSION`
    - `PARSER_DIAGNOSTIC_SCHEMA_VERSION`
    - `PARSER_DIAGNOSTIC_HASH_ALGORITHM`
    - `PARSER_DIAGNOSTIC_HASH_PREFIX`
  - Extended `ParseErrorCode` with deterministic metadata APIs:
    - `ALL`, `as_str()`, `stable_diagnostic_code()`, `diagnostic_category()`, `diagnostic_severity()`, `diagnostic_message_template(...)`
  - Added diagnostics taxonomy model:
    - `ParseDiagnosticCategory`, `ParseDiagnosticSeverity`
    - `ParseDiagnosticRule`, `ParseDiagnosticTaxonomy::{v1,taxonomy_version,rule_for}`
  - Added normalized diagnostics envelope model:
    - `ParseDiagnosticEnvelope` with canonical serialization + hash (`canonical_value`, `canonical_bytes`, `canonical_hash`)
    - `normalize_parse_error(&ParseError)`
    - `ParseError::normalized_diagnostic()` convenience API
  - Added canonical witness support (`ParseFailureWitness::canonical_value`) and `ParseBudgetKind::as_str()`.
  - Added/updated parser unit tests for:
    - taxonomy metadata stability
    - taxonomy uniqueness/completeness
    - normalization determinism across raw message variance
    - budget witness normalization semantics
    - envelope serde/hash stability

- `crates/franken-engine/tests/parser_trait_ast.rs`
  - Added diagnostics metadata stability test.
  - Added pinned normalized-diagnostic hash vectors:
    - empty source -> `sha256:0f8535a4bba696fd0f0fc51bbe13ed8c9e4a5d1e8dd8f84acb7ce228ad17f68a`
    - token-budget exceeded -> `sha256:443c9fe5a7218a6bd060824b175744a1d4cb36de120e0bbecb70f13eea5a29e5`

- `docs/PARSER_DIAGNOSTICS_TAXONOMY.md`
  - Added v1 contract doc: taxonomy mapping table, normalization rules, canonical envelope shape, hash formula, compatibility policy, replay command.

- `README.md`
  - Added diagnostics taxonomy/normalization contract references + rch replay command in parser phase0 section.

## Validation (heavy cargo via `rch`)
- PASS: `cargo test -p frankenengine-engine --test parser_trait_ast`
  - includes new diagnostics metadata and pinned hash-vector checks (13/13 pass).
- PASS: `cargo check --all-targets`
- PASS: `cargo test` (workspace-wide)
- FAIL (pre-existing unrelated): `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `crates/franken-engine/src/checkpoint.rs:744:5` duplicated `#[test]` attribute
  - `crates/franken-engine/src/obligation_leak_policy.rs:471:5` duplicated `#[test]` attribute
  - `crates/franken-engine/src/obligation_leak_policy.rs:514:33` missing method `create_event` on `LeakHandler`
- FAIL (pre-existing unrelated broad drift): `cargo fmt --check`
  - widespread formatting differences across many non-lane files.

## Notes
- All CPU-intensive cargo/test/clippy/check/fmt commands were executed through `rch`.
- No destructive commands used.","created_at":"2026-02-25T02:29:18Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2mds.1.1.4","title":"[PSRP-01.4] Expand normative/adversarial corpus + reducer promotion policy","description":"## Context
Grow corpus breadth and define automatic promotion rules for minimized regressions into permanent fixtures.

## Work
- Expand normative + adversarial corpora.
- Automate reducer promotion with provenance.
- Require compatibility with both AST contract and diagnostics normalization outputs.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.","acceptance_criteria":"Corpus and reducer policy are automated, documented, and validated by deterministic promotion tests; unit tests and e2e corpus sweeps are comprehensive; structured logs retain provenance from raw failure to promoted fixture.

Required verification addendum: include focused unit tests and, for any cross-subsystem behavior, deterministic e2e scenarios with structured logs/traces and one-command replay instructions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"QuietBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:02.579643657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T06:25:38.799136339Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T06:25:38.799110140Z","close_reason":"Implemented adversarial corpus + reducer promotion policy/contracts with deterministic parity/replay coverage; focused lanes pass; required repo gates attempted and recorded with pre-existing+infra blockers.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser-frontier","parser-supremacy","world-class-parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:02.579643657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:55:32.892590752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:16.702275162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":268,"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented the full `bd-2mds.1.1.4` scope for corpus expansion + deterministic reducer promotion policy.

## Delivered

1. Added adversarial corpus fixture catalog with deterministic expected parse/diagnostic contracts:
- `crates/franken-engine/tests/fixtures/parser_phase0_adversarial_fixtures.json`

2. Added reducer promotion policy artifact (schema/versioned policy contract):
- `crates/franken-engine/tests/fixtures/parser_reducer_promotion_policy.json`

3. Added deterministic promotion/parity test lane:
- `crates/franken-engine/tests/parser_corpus_promotion_policy.rs`
- Tests cover:
  - normative + adversarial promotion contract
  - deterministic receipt hashing
  - adversarial diagnostic drift detection

4. Wired new test lane into parser gate script:
- `scripts/run_parser_phase0_gate.sh`

5. Added deterministic reducer-promotion gate + replay lane with structured logging:
- `scripts/run_parser_reducer_promotion_gate.sh`
- `scripts/e2e/parser_reducer_promotion_replay.sh`
- Structured artifact outputs include run manifest + events jsonl + commands transcript.

6. Updated docs:
- `docs/PARSER_GRAMMAR_CLOSURE_BACKLOG.md`
- `README.md`

## Focused verification (PASS)

- `rch exec -- env RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_engine_parser_reducer_promotion cargo test -p frankenengine-engine --test parser_corpus_promotion_policy -- --nocapture`
  - Result: `3 passed; 0 failed`

- `./scripts/run_parser_reducer_promotion_gate.sh ci`
  - Result: pass
  - Manifest: `artifacts/parser_reducer_promotion/20260225T060127Z/run_manifest.json`
  - Events: `artifacts/parser_reducer_promotion/20260225T060127Z/events.jsonl`

- `./scripts/e2e/parser_reducer_promotion_replay.sh`
  - Result: pass
  - Manifest: `artifacts/parser_reducer_promotion/20260225T060544Z/run_manifest.json`
  - Events: `artifacts/parser_reducer_promotion/20260225T060544Z/events.jsonl`

- `./scripts/run_parser_phase0_gate.sh ci`
  - Artifacts emitted:
    - `artifacts/parser_phase0_gate/20260225T060815Z/run_manifest.json`
    - `artifacts/parser_phase0_gate/20260225T060815Z/events.jsonl`

## Required repo gates (attempted via rch)

1. `cargo check --all-targets` -> **FAIL**
- Pre-existing compile breaks in `crates/franken-engine/src/fleet_convergence.rs` (undefined names + type mismatches).

2. `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` -> **FAIL**
- Broad pre-existing lint debt across many files (duplicate macro attributes, too-many-arguments, unnecessary casts, vec-init patterns, etc).

3. `cargo fmt --check` -> **FAIL**
- Broad pre-existing formatting drift across many unrelated files.

4. `cargo test` -> **FAIL / infra-blocked**
- `rch` remote worker disk exhaustion (`No space left on device`), with link/rustc failures under exhausted storage.
- `rch` then attempted local fallback compilation; fallback was terminated to honor the project rule that CPU-heavy cargo ops must run via `rch`.

## Notes

- Changes for this bead are localized to parser corpus/promotion policy + gate/replay/docs surfaces.
- No destructive commands run.
","created_at":"2026-02-25T06:25:33Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2mds.1.10","title":"[PSRP-10] User-Facing Parser Excellence (Diagnostics, Recovery, Ergonomics)","description":"## Context
Mission: ensure the parser is not only fast/correct, but materially better for users through clearer diagnostics, robust recovery behavior, and ergonomic/stable integration surfaces.

Why this matters:
- Users experience parser quality through error messages, resilience after malformed code, and API usability.
- A parser can win microbenchmarks yet still fail real developer workflows if diagnostics and recovery are weak.

Scope and intent:
- Establish a diagnostics quality rubric and enforce it with golden tests.
- Improve error recovery/resynchronization so downstream tooling gets useful partial structure.
- Harden parser APIs/contracts and migration ergonomics for consumers.
- Publish operator/developer runbooks that make replay and troubleshooting straightforward.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.

## Success Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Diagnostics quality rubric (clarity, location accuracy, fix hint usefulness, determinism) is codified and test-enforced.
2. Recovery/resynchronization behavior passes adversarial e2e scenarios while preserving deterministic semantics and safety.
3. Parser APIs are versioned, documented, compatibility-tested, and accompanied by migration guidance where needed.
4. Operator/developer runbooks provide deterministic troubleshooting workflows with rich logs and replay commands.
5. User-impact metrics (diagnostic quality and recovery success rates) are tracked and referenced in PSRP-08 evidence bundles.
6. User-facing quality is backed by both focused unit tests and end-to-end workflow scripts.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:43.453996724Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T21:56:43.391716695Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser-frontier","parser-supremacy","world-class-parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:43.453996724Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2mds.1.10.1","title":"[PSRP-10.1] Define diagnostics quality rubric + golden tests for actionable errors","description":"## Context
Define and operationalize a diagnostics quality rubric so error outputs are consistently useful to developers and operators.

Rubric dimensions:
- location precision
- message clarity and specificity
- actionable fix hints
- deterministic wording/codes across runs

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.","acceptance_criteria":"Golden diagnostic tests cover major grammar families and edge cases; rubric scores and deterministic snapshots are produced per release candidate via both targeted unit tests and user-journey e2e scenarios with structured logs; baseline-vs-current deltas are computed and regression thresholds trigger automated alarms.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlDesert","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:53.205901148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T09:15:48.383695782Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T09:15:48.383675063Z","close_reason":"Implemented diagnostics quality rubric doc+fixture+golden tests+rch runner; focused rch test lane passing; workspace-wide check/clippy/fmt blockers are unrelated external drift","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser-frontier","parser-supremacy","world-class-parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:40:21.672978704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:53.205901148Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:40:21.841609949Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:46:43.131294758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.9.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:00:14.111016962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":286,"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (PearlDesert) — diagnostics quality rubric scaffold

Implemented in this pass:
1) New diagnostics rubric spec doc:
   - `docs/PARSER_DIAGNOSTICS_QUALITY_RUBRIC.md`
   - defines schema/version, dimensions, golden-family requirements, baseline-delta regression policy, structured log contract, deterministic `rch` execution, and artifact verification.
2) New deterministic rubric fixture:
   - `crates/franken-engine/tests/fixtures/parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric_v1.json`
   - includes weighted dimensions, baseline scores, required structured log keys, and golden parser diagnostics cases spanning `input|goal|syntax|resource`.
3) New focused integration test target:
   - `crates/franken-engine/tests/parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric.rs`
   - validates rubric doc/fixture contracts
   - evaluates golden cases via canonical parser diagnostics envelopes
   - computes deterministic per-dimension/composite scores
   - enforces baseline-vs-current regression bounds
   - verifies deterministic structured user-journey logs + replay commands.
4) New `rch` gate runner:
   - `scripts/run_parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric.sh`
   - modes: `check|test|clippy|ci`
   - emits run manifest/events/commands + schema-validated log event.

Validation status:
- PASS: `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/franken-engine/tests/parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric.rs`
- PASS: fixture JSON validation (`jq -e`) + script syntax validation (`bash -n`)
- rch gate attempted:
  - `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_parser_diagnostics_quality ./scripts/run_parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric.sh check`
  - artifact bundle emitted at `artifacts/parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric/20260225T085216Z/`
  - run outcome `fail` due unrelated workspace compile drift in `crates/franken-engine/src/parallel_parser.rs` (`E0425` unresolved `merge_witness`), not in rubric files.

Blocker summary:
- Focused cargo verification for this bead currently blocked by unrelated compile breakage in parser parallel lane.
","created_at":"2026-02-25T08:55:07Z"},{"id":291,"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.10.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (PearlDesert) — diagnostics rubric fixture correction + focused rch reruns

Follow-up fix in this pass:
- Updated syntax golden case in `crates/franken-engine/tests/fixtures/parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric_v1.json`:
  - replaced non-failing scaffold case (`if (true) {}`) with guaranteed unsupported syntax case under module goal (`import`).
  - keeps major-family coverage (`input|goal|syntax|resource`) while aligning with current parser scaffold semantics.

Why this was needed:
- Focused `rch` test run initially failed because parser currently accepts many unsupported constructs as `Expression::Raw`; the previous syntax case produced success, not diagnostic.

Validation reruns (rch-only heavy cargo):
- PASS: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_parser_diagnostics_quality ./scripts/run_parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric.sh test`
  - artifact bundle: `artifacts/parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric/20260225T090410Z/`
- PASS (previous run): `... run_parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric.sh check`
  - artifact bundle: `artifacts/parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric/20260225T085847Z/`
- FAIL (external unrelated drift): `... run_parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric.sh check`
  - now blocked by unrelated compile error in `crates/franken-engine/src/governance_mechanism.rs` (`E0061` in `total_loss()` call)
  - artifact bundle: `artifacts/parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric/20260225T091208Z/`
- FAIL (external unrelated drift): `... run_parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric.sh clippy`
  - unrelated warnings/lints in `incentive_governance_mechanism.rs`, `observability_probe_design.rs`, `cut_line_automation.rs`, `succinct_witness_compiler.rs`
  - artifact bundle: `artifacts/parser_diagnostics_quality_rubric/20260225T090814Z/`
- FAIL (external unrelated drift): `rch exec -- env RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly cargo fmt --check`
  - unrelated formatting diffs (e.g. `crates/franken-engine/src/governance_mechanism.rs`, `observability_probe_design.rs`).

Bead-specific status:
- rubric/doc/fixture/tests/script lane is functionally green on focused target test and emits deterministic manifest/events contracts.
- remaining failures are workspace-level drift outside this bead scope.
","created_at":"2026-02-25T09:15:42Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2mds.1.10.2","title":"[PSRP-10.2] Harden error recovery/resynchronization with adversarial e2e validation","description":"## Context
Strengthen parser error recovery/resynchronization behavior so users retain useful AST/diagnostics even under malformed or partially-edited code.

Include adversarial and incremental-edit scenarios to mimic real editor workflows.

## Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-2mds.1.8","title":"[PSRP-08] Supremacy Readiness Gate (Correctness + Performance + Determinism)","description":"## Context
Mission: define and enforce the final evidence bar before any world-leading parser claim is accepted.

Why this matters:
- This closes the gap between engineering progress and claim integrity.
- It prevents premature declaration without full, reproducible proof.

Intent:
- Fail closed unless correctness, performance, reproducibility, testing rigor, and user-facing quality all meet contract.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.

## Success Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Supremacy criteria and mandatory evidence package are explicit and machine-checkable.
2. Correctness gate requires zero unresolved high-severity differential drifts.
3. Performance gate requires threshold wins versus Boa/peers on declared quantiles/workloads.
4. Comprehensive unit/property/regression/e2e suites with structured logs are green and linked.
5. User-facing quality signals (diagnostics and recovery) meet published thresholds.
6. Final readiness report includes risk register, rollback posture, and third-party rerun evidence.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:01.157422311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T21:56:43.059903668Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser-frontier","parser-supremacy","world-class-parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:01.157422311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2mds.1.8.1","title":"[PSRP-08.1] Define supremacy criteria and mandatory evidence contract","description":"## Context
Define the supremacy contract early, then keep it versioned and machine-evaluable as implementation evidence accumulates.

Intent:
- Publish an initial criteria contract early enough to guide implementation decisions.
- Make criteria updates explicit, auditable, and diffable rather than implicit scope drift.
- Ensure the gate can be executed automatically from artifact bundles.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.","acceptance_criteria":"1. A versioned supremacy criteria spec (v0+) is published before downstream gate closure and covers correctness, determinism, performance, reproducibility, verification rigor, and user-facing quality.
2. Criteria are machine-checkable via an automated evaluator with focused unit tests for each rule class.
3. A deterministic e2e gate simulation validates evaluator behavior against representative artifact bundles.
4. Criteria changes require logged rationale, impact assessment, and compatibility notes in a criteria changelog.
5. Structured gate logs include: `run_id`, `criteria_version`, `git_sha`, `artifact_bundle_id`, `verdict`, and replay command.

Required verification addendum: include focused unit tests and, for any cross-subsystem behavior, deterministic e2e scenarios with structured logs/traces and one-command replay instructions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlDesert","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:11.610155966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T09:59:50.145373776Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T09:59:50.145350964Z","close_reason":"Supremacy  {"id":"bd-2mds.1.8.2","title":"[PSRP-08.2] Correctness gate: zero unresolved high-severity drifts","description":"## Context
Execute the correctness promotion gate as a fail-closed, evidence-backed decision.

Intent:
- Combine differential drift closure with deterministic semantic-equivalence evidence.
- Prevent promotion when unresolved correctness risk remains, even if performance looks good.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.","acceptance_criteria":"1. High-severity unresolved differential drift count is zero, or any exception is formally waived with time-bounded remediation and explicit owner sign-off.
2. Correctness evidence includes deterministic passes for differential harness, event-to-AST equivalence, parallel fallback parity, and parser-oracle semantic checks.
3. Gate execution is scripted, deterministic, and reproducible via one-command replay.
4. Gate logs are structured and include drift inventory, waiver inventory, failing fixture IDs, and replay pointers.
5. Correctness gate cannot pass if required unit/e2e suites are red or missing artifacts.

Required verification addendum: include focused unit tests and, for any cross-subsystem behavior, deterministic e2e scenarios with structured logs/traces and one-command replay instructions.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacGorge","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:11.956781895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T21:03:01.432996495Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser-frontier","parser-supremacy","world-class-parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1b70","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:40:25.255305022Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.10.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:46:40.923452369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.2.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:06:23.754320018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.4.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:06:23.040998252Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.5.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T22:09:05.184459445Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:46:40.576845953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:11.956781895Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:20.065071220Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:46:40.750388577Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":418,"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.2","author":" -{"id":"bd-2mds.1.8.3","title":"[PSRP-08.3] Performance gate: beat Boa/peers on defined quantiles","description":"## Context
Execute the performance promotion gate with reproducible methodology and anti-p-hacking protections.

Intent:
- Require statistically credible wins over Boa/peer parsers on declared workloads.
- Prevent selective benchmark cherry-picking through fixed protocol and artifact completeness.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Declared workload/quantile thresholds versus Boa/peers are met with reproducible reruns using the published benchmark protocol.
2. Results include variance/confidence reporting and clearly identify hardware/feature configurations.
3. Performance wins must coexist with green correctness and determinism prerequisites.
4. Gate logs include corpus IDs, quantiles, sample counts, confidence stats, and full replay commands.
5. Gate fails closed on missing telemetry artifacts, protocol drift, or non-reproducible wins.

Required verification addendum: include focused unit tests and, for any cross-subsystem behavior, deterministic e2e scenarios with structured logs/traces and one-command replay instructions.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:12.303298790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T21:55:33.423597175Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser-frontier","parser-supremacy","world-class-parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.6.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:55:33.423549276Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:40:25.762856242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:46:41.102474939Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:32:12.303298790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:40:25.593509695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.8.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.9.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:46:41.272495753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2mds.1.8.4","title":"[PSRP-08.4] Final readiness report, risk register, and rollback posture","description":"## Context
Produce the final readiness dossier that synthesizes technical evidence, user impact, residual risk, and rollback posture before any supremacy declaration.

Intent:
- Make the final decision legible to future operators without reconstructing tribal context.
- Preserve a clear rollback and contingency path if post-declaration evidence regresses.

 {"id":"bd-2mds.1.9","title":"[PSRP-09] Verification Matrix, Test Pyramid, and Evidence Logging Hardening","description":"## Context
Mission: make parser quality measurable and enforceable through a full verification pyramid that includes unit, property, regression, and end-to-end validation with deterministic evidence logs.

Why this matters:
- Architecture and performance work can regress correctness unless guarded by deep test coverage.
- E2E scripts with rich logging are the fastest way to root-cause failures across parser, lexer, event IR, and parallel scheduler layers.
- Claims of world-leading reliability require auditable proof artifacts, not just green local runs.

Scope and intent:
- Define verification architecture and quantitative coverage goals per parser subsystem.
- Build comprehensive unit/property/regression suites tied to drift categories.
- Implement deterministic e2e scripts that emit structured logs, replay manifests, and diff-friendly artifacts.
- Enforce CI quality gates and long-horizon artifact retention so regressions are explainable weeks later.

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.

## Success Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Verification architecture documents a test pyramid with explicit coverage targets for lexer, parser, event IR, parallel scheduler/merge, diagnostics, and API boundaries.
2. Every subsystem has comprehensive unit + property + regression tests with deterministic fixtures and reproducible failure minimization.
3. E2E scenario harness covers success, malformed input, adversarial input, cancellation, timeout, and fallback paths with structured run logs.
4. CI gates fail closed on missing coverage/quality thresholds and retain enough artifacts/logs for replay-based triage.
5. Test and e2e evidence is linked into PSRP-08 readiness gating without manual reconstruction.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:43.289683156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:42:35.079801782Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:42:35.079777156Z","close_reason":"All PSRP-09 child beads closed with deterministic evidence lanes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser-frontier","parser-supremacy","world-class-parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:43.289683156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2mds.1.9.1","title":"[PSRP-09.1] Define parser verification architecture and coverage map (unit/property/e2e)","description":"## Context
Design the verification architecture as executable policy: what is tested at each layer, what confidence each layer provides, and which artifacts are mandatory for debugging and audits.

Deliverables include:
- subsystem-by-subsystem coverage matrix (lexer, parser core, event IR, materializer, parallel scheduler/merge, diagnostics, API)
- unit/property/e2e boundary definitions
- minimum deterministic logging fields for every test and benchmark run
- ownership + escalation model for failing coverage targets

## Acceptance Criteria
See acceptance criteria field for explicit completion gates.","acceptance_criteria":"A versioned coverage map exists with explicit quantitative targets, ownership, and fail-closed thresholds; CI validates policy conformance automatically; policy-checker unit tests and pilot e2e verification runs prove enforceability; and structured log schema is fixed, including `run_id`, `seed`, `git_sha`, `corpus_id`, `input_sha256`, `arch`, `feature_flags`, `worker_count`, `elapsed_ms`, `peak_rss_bytes`, `verdict`, and replay command.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:52.540553989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:12:42.146131085Z","closed_at":"2026-02-24T22:12:42.146109124Z","close_reason":"Published parser verification architecture and coverage map contract (new PARSER_VERIFICATION_ARCHITECTURE doc), defined mandatory logging schema keys, and documented ownership/escalation + execution contract; updated parser frontier env contract to reference and enforce the verification schema.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parser-frontier","parser-supremacy","world-class-parser"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2mds.1.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:52.540553989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":238,"issue_id":"bd-2mds.1.9.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented  verification architecture deliverables.

Files:
- `docs/PARSER_VERIFICATION_ARCHITECTURE.md` (new)
- `docs/PARSER_FRONTIER_ENV_CONTRACT.md` (updated)

Delivered content:
1. Verification layer model (`L0` compile/lint through `L5` evidence/audit) with fail semantics.
2. Subsystem-by-subsystem coverage matrix for lexer, parser core, AST/event materialization, diagnostics/recovery, arena determinism, parallel parser/merge, and oracle harness; each row maps to concrete src/tests/scripts/artifacts in this repo.
3. Explicit unit/property/e2e boundary definitions to prevent overlap and blind spots.
4. Mandatory parser verification logging schema v1 fields (`trace_id`, `decision_id`, `policy_id`, `component`, `event`, `outcome`, `error_code`) plus recommended parser fields.
5. Ownership plus escalation model for coverage failures including failure taxonomy and coordination workflow.
6. Execution contract with CI/e2e entrypoints and explicit `rch` requirement for heavy cargo paths.

Notes:
- This bead is documentation/contract architecture; no parser runtime source changes were made in this slice.
- Updated env-contract doc now references the architecture contract and aligns required event keys.
